fields.name: LPMS
fields.description: low-power mode selection This bit defines the Deepsleep mode.

enum_values.name: StopMode
enum_values.description: Keeps Stop mode when entering DeepSleep

enum_values.name: StandbyMode
enum_values.description: Allows Standby mode when entering DeepSleep

fields.name: SVOS
fields.description: system Stop mode voltage scaling selection These bits control the V CORE voltage level in system Stop mode, to obtain the best trade-off between power consumption and performance.

enum_values.name: Scale5
enum_values.description: SVOS5 scale 5

enum_values.name: Scale4
enum_values.description: SVOS4 scale 4

enum_values.name: Scale3
enum_values.description: SVOS3 scale 3

fields.name: CSSF
fields.description: clear Standby and Stop flags (always read as 0) This bit is cleared to 0 by hardware.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: STOPF and SBF flags cleared

fields.name: FLPS
fields.description: Flash memory low-power mode in Stop mode This bit is used to obtain the best trade-off between low-power consumption and restart time when exiting from Stop mode. When it is set, the Flash memory enters low-power mode when the CPU domain is in Stop mode. Note: When system enters stop mode with SVOS5 enabled, Flash memory is automatically forced in low-power mode.

enum_values.name: NormalMode
enum_values.description: Flash memory remains in normal mode when the system enters Stop mode

enum_values.name: LowPowerMode
enum_values.description: Flash memory enters low-power mode when the system enters Stop mode

fields.name: BOOSTE
fields.description: analog switch V BOOST control This bit enables the booster to guarantee the analog switch AC performance when the V DD supply voltage is below 2.7 V (reduction of the total harmonic distortion to have the same switch performance over the full supply voltage range) The V DD supply voltage can be monitored through the PVD and the PLS bits.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Booster disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Booster enabled if analog voltage ready (AVD_READY = 1)

fields.name: AVD_READY
fields.description: analog voltage ready This bit is only used when the analog switch boost needs to be enabled (see BOOSTE bit). It must be set by software when the expected V DDA analog supply level is available. The correct analog supply level is indicated by the AVDO bit (PWR_VMSR register) after setting the AVDEN bit (PWR_VMCR register) and selecting the supply level to be monitored (ALS bits).

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: Peripheral analog voltage VDDA not ready (default)

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: Peripheral analog voltage VDDA ready

fields.name: SRAM3SO
fields.description: AHB SRAM3 shut-off in Stop mode.

fields.name: SRAM2_16SO
fields.description: AHB SRAM2 16-Kbyte shut-off in Stop mode.

enum_values.name: Kept
enum_values.description: AHB RAM2 content is kept in Stop mode

enum_values.name: Lost
enum_values.description: AHB RAM2 content is lost in Stop mode

fields.name: SRAM2_48SO
fields.description: AHB SRAM2 48-Kbyte shut-off in Stop mode.

fields.name: SRAM1SO
fields.description: AHB SRAM1 shut-off in Stop mode

enum_values.name: Kept
enum_values.description: AHB RAM1 content is kept in Stop mode

enum_values.name: Lost
enum_values.description: AHB RAM1 content is lost in Stop mode

fields.name: STOPF
fields.description: Stop flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by any reset or by setting the CSSF bit.

enum_values.name: NoStopMode
enum_values.description: System has not been in stop mode

enum_values.name: StopModePreviouslyEntered
enum_values.description: System has been in Stop mode

fields.name: SBF
fields.description: System standby flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a POR or by setting the CSSF bit.

enum_values.name: NoStandbyMode
enum_values.description: System has not been in standby mode

enum_values.name: StandbyModePreviouslyEntered
enum_values.description: System has been in Standby mode

fields.name: VOS
fields.description: voltage scaling selection according to performance These bits control the V CORE voltage level and allow to obtain the best trade-off between power consumption and performance: - In bypass mode, these bits must also be set according to the external provided core voltage level and related performance. - When increasing the performance, the voltage scaling must be changed before increasing the system frequency. - When decreasing performance, the system frequency must first be decreased before changing the voltage scaling.

enum_values.name: VOS3
enum_values.description: Scale 3 (default)

enum_values.name: VOS1
enum_values.description: Scale 1

enum_values.name: VOS2
enum_values.description: Scale 2

enum_values.name: VOS0
enum_values.description: Scale 0

fields.name: VOSRDY
fields.description: Ready bit for V CORE voltage scaling output selection.

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: Not ready, voltage level below VOS selected level

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: Ready, voltage level at or above VOS selected level

fields.name: ACTVOSRDY
fields.description: Voltage level ready for currently used VOS

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: VCORE is above or below the current voltage scaling provided by ACTVOS[1:0]

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: VCORE is equal to the current voltage scaling provided by ACTVOS[1:0]

fields.name: ACTVOS
fields.description: voltage output scaling currently applied to V CORE This field provides the last VOS value.

enum_values.name: VOS3
enum_values.description: VOS3 (lowest power)

enum_values.name: VOS2
enum_values.description: VOS2

enum_values.name: VOS1
enum_values.description: VOS1

enum_values.name: VOS0
enum_values.description: VOS0 (highest frequency)

fields.name: BREN
fields.description: Backup RAM retention in Standby and V BAT modes When this bit set, the backup regulator (used to maintain the backup RAM content in Standby and V BAT modes) is enabled. If BREN is cleared, the backup regulator is switched off. The backup RAM can still be used in Run and Stop modes. However its content is lost in Standby and V BAT modes. If BREN is set, the application must wait till the backup regulator ready flag (BRRDY) is set to indicate that the data written into the SRAM is maintained in Standby and V BAT modes.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Backup regulator enabled; backup RAM content lost in Standby and VBAT modes

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Backup regulator disabled; backup RAM content preserved in Standby and VBAT modes

fields.name: MONEN
fields.description: Backup domain voltage and temperature monitoring enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Backup domain voltage and temperature monitoring disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Backup domain voltage and temperature monitoring enabled

fields.name: VBE
fields.description: V BAT charging enable Note: Reset only by POR,.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: VBAT battery charging disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: VBAT battery charging enabled

fields.name: VBRS
fields.description: V BAT charging resistor selection

enum_values.name: Charge5k
enum_values.description: Charge VBAT through a 5 kΩ resistor

enum_values.name: Charge1k5
enum_values.description: Charge VBAT through a 1.5 kΩ resistor

fields.name: DBP
fields.description: Disable Backup domain write protection In reset state, all registers and SRAM in Backup domain are protected against parasitic write access. This bit must be set to enable write access to these registers.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Write access to backup domain disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Write access to backup domain enabled

fields.name: BRRDY
fields.description: backup regulator ready This bit is set by hardware to indicate that the backup regulator is ready.

enum_values.name: NotReady
enum_values.description: Backup regulator not ready

enum_values.name: Ready
enum_values.description: Backup regulator ready

fields.name: VBATL
fields.description: V BAT level monitoring versus low threshold

enum_values.name: AboveThreshold
enum_values.description: Above low threshold level

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: Equal to or below low threshold level

fields.name: VBATH
fields.description: V BAT level monitoring versus high threshold

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: Below high threshold level

enum_values.name: AboveThreshold
enum_values.description: Equal to or Above high threshold level

fields.name: TEMPL
fields.description: temperature level monitoring versus low threshold

fields.name: TEMPH
fields.description: temperature level monitoring versus high threshold

registers.name: UCPDR
registers.description: PWR USB Type-C power delivery register

fields.name: UCPD_DBDIS
fields.description: USB Type-C and power delivery dead battery disable After exiting reset, the USB Type-C 'dead battery' behavior is enabled, which may have a pull-down effect on CC1 and CC2 pins. It is recommended to disable it in all case, either to stop this pull-down or to hand over control to the UCPD (which should therefore be initialized before doing the disable).

fields.name: UCPD_STBY
fields.description: USB Type-c and Power delivery Standby mode When set, this bit is used to memorize the UCPD configuration in Standby mode. This bit must be written to 1 just before entering Standby mode when using UCPD, and it must be written to 0 after exiting the standby mode and before writing any UCPD register.

fields.name: BYPASS
fields.description: power management unit bypass

enum_values.name: InternalRegulator
enum_values.description: Power management unit normal operation. Use the internal regulator.

enum_values.name: Bypassed
enum_values.description: Power management unit bypassed. Use the external power.

fields.name: LDOEN
fields.description: LDO enable The value is set by hardware when the package uses the LDO regulator.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Package does not use LDO regulator

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Package uses LDO regulator

fields.name: SMPSEN
fields.description: SMPS enable The value is set by hardware when the package uses the SMPS regulator.

fields.name: PVDE
fields.description: PVD enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: PVD Disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: PVD Enabled

fields.name: PLS
fields.description: programmable voltage detector (PVD) level selection These bits select the voltage threshold detected by the PVD.

enum_values.name: PvdLevel0
enum_values.description: PVD level0 (VPVD0 around 1.95 V)

enum_values.name: PvdLevel1
enum_values.description: PVD level1 (VPVD1 around 2.1 V)

enum_values.name: PvdLevel2
enum_values.description: PVD level2 (VPVD2 around 2.25 V)

enum_values.name: PvdLevel3
enum_values.description: PVD level3 (VPVD3 around 2.4 V)

enum_values.name: PvdLevel4
enum_values.description: PVD level4 (VPVD4 around 2.55 V)

enum_values.name: PvdLevel5
enum_values.description: PVD level5 (VPVD5 around 2.7 V)

enum_values.name: PvdLevel6
enum_values.description: PVD level6 (VPVD6 around 2.85 V)

enum_values.name: PvdIn
enum_values.description: PVD_IN pin

fields.name: AVDEN
fields.description: peripheral voltage monitor on V DDA enable

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: Peripheral voltage monitor on VDDA disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: Peripheral voltage monitor on VDDA enabled

fields.name: ALS
fields.description: analog voltage detector (AVD) level selection These bits select the voltage threshold detected by the AVD.

enum_values.name: AvdLevel0
enum_values.description: AVD level0 (VAVD0 around 1.7 V)

enum_values.name: AvdLevel1
enum_values.description: AVD level1 (VAVD1 around 2.1 V)

enum_values.name: AvdLevel2
enum_values.description: AVD level2 (VAVD2 around 2.5 V)

enum_values.name: AvdLevel3
enum_values.description: AVD level3 (VAVD3 around 2.8 V)

fields.name: USB33DEN
fields.description: V DDUSB voltage level detector enable

fields.name: USB33SV
fields.description: independent USB supply valid This bit is used to validate the V DDUSB supply for electrical and logical isolation purpose. Setting this bit is mandatory to use the USBFS peripheral. If V DDUSB is not always present in the application, the V DDUSB voltage monitor can be used to determine whether this supply is ready or not.

fields.name: AVDO
fields.description: analog voltage detector output on V DDA This bit is set and cleared by hardware. It is valid only if AVD on VDDA is enabled by the AVDEN bit. Note: Since the AVD is disabled in Standby mode, this bit is equal to 0 after standby or reset until the AVDEN bit is set.

enum_values.name: AboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDA is equal or higher than the AVD threshold selected with the ALS[2:0] bits

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDA is lower than the AVD threshold selected with the ALS[2:0] bits

fields.name: VDDIO2RDY
fields.description: voltage detector output on V DDIO2 This bit is set and cleared by hardware.

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 is below the threshold of the VDDIO2 voltage monitor

enum_values.name: AboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDDIO2 is equal or above the threshold of the VDDIO2 voltage monitor

fields.name: PVDO
fields.description: programmable voltage detect output This bit is set and cleared by hardware. It is valid only if the PVD has been enabled by the PVDE bit. Note: Since the PVD is disabled in Standby mode, this bit is equal to 0 after Standby or reset until the PVDE bit is set.

enum_values.name: AboveThreshold
enum_values.description: VDD is equal or higher than the PVD threshold selected through the PLS[2:0] bits.

enum_values.name: BelowThreshold
enum_values.description: VDD is lower than the PVD threshold selected through the PLS[2:0] bits

fields.name: USB33RDY
fields.description: V DDUSB ready

fields.name: CWUF1
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

enum_values.name: Clear
enum_values.description: Writing 1 clears the WUFx wakeup pin flag (bit is cleared to 0 by hardware)

fields.name: CWUF2
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

fields.name: CWUF3
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

fields.name: CWUF4
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

fields.name: CWUF5
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

fields.name: CWUF6
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

fields.name: CWUF7
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

fields.name: CWUF8
fields.description: clear wakeup pin flag for WUFx These bits are always read as 0.

fields.name: WUF1
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

enum_values.name: NoEventOccurred
enum_values.description: No wakeup event occurred

enum_values.name: EventOccurred
enum_values.description: A wakeup event received from WUFx pin

fields.name: WUF2
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

fields.name: WUF3
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

fields.name: WUF4
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

fields.name: WUF5
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

fields.name: WUF6
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

fields.name: WUF7
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

fields.name: WUF8
fields.description: wakeup pin WUFx flag This bit is set by hardware and cleared only by a RESET pin or by setting the CWUFx bit in PWR_WUSCR register.

fields.name: WUPEN1
fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: An event on WUPx pin does not wakeup the system from Standby mode

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: A rising or falling edge on WUPx pin wakes up the system from Standby mode

fields.name: WUPEN2
fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

fields.name: WUPEN3
fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

fields.name: WUPEN4
fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

fields.name: WUPEN6
fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

fields.name: WUPEN7
fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

fields.name: WUPEN8
fields.description: enable wakeup pin WUPx These bits are set and cleared by software. Note: an additional wakeup event is detected if WUPx pin is enabled (by setting the WUPENx bit) when WUPx pin level is already high when WUPPx selects rising edge, or low when WUPPx selects falling edge.

fields.name: WUPP1
fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

enum_values.name: HighLevel
enum_values.description: Detection on high level

enum_values.name: LowLevel
enum_values.description: Detection on low level

fields.name: WUPP2
fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

fields.name: WUPP3
fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

fields.name: WUPP4
fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

fields.name: WUPP6
fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

fields.name: WUPP7
fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

fields.name: WUPP8
fields.description: wakeup pin polarity bit for WUPx These bits define the polarity used for event detection on WUPx external wakeup pin.

fields.name: WUPPUPD1
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

enum_values.name: NoPull
enum_values.description: No pull-up or pull-down

enum_values.name: PullUp
enum_values.description: Pull-up

enum_values.name: PullDown
enum_values.description: Pull-down

fields.name: WUPPUPD2
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

fields.name: WUPPUPD3
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

fields.name: WUPPUPD4
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

fields.name: WUPPUPD5
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

fields.name: WUPPUPD6
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

fields.name: WUPPUPD7
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

fields.name: WUPPUPD8
fields.description: wakeup pin pull configuration for WKUPx These bits define the I/O pad pull configuration used when WUPENx = 1. The associated GPIO port pull configuration must be set to the same value or to 00. The wakeup pin pull configuration is kept in Standby mode.

fields.name: IORETEN
fields.description: IO retention enable: When entering into standby mode, the output is sampled, and apply to the output IO during the standby power mode. Note: the IO state is not retained if the DBG_STANDBY bit is set in DBGMCU_CR register.

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: IO Retention mode is disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: IO Retention mode is enabled

fields.name: JTAGIORETEN
fields.description: IO retention enable for JTAG IOs when entering into standby mode, the output is sampled, and apply to the output IO during the standby power mode

enum_values.name: Disabled
enum_values.description: IO Retention mode is disabled

enum_values.name: Enabled
enum_values.description: IO Retention mode is enabled

fields.name: WUP1SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: WUP2SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: WUP3SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: WUP4SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: WUP5SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: WUP6SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: WUP7SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: WUP8SEC
fields.description: WUPx secure protection

fields.name: RETSEC
fields.description: retention secure protection

fields.name: LPMSEC
fields.description: low-power modes secure protection

fields.name: SCMSEC
fields.description: supply configuration and monitoring secure protection.

fields.name: VBSEC
fields.description: backup domain secure protection

fields.name: VUSBSEC
fields.description: voltage USB secure protection

fields.name: SPRIV
fields.description: PWR secure functions privilege configuration Set and reset by software. This bit can be written only by a secure privileged access.

fields.name: NSPRIV
fields.description: PWR non-secure functions privilege configuration Set and reset by software. This bit can be written only by privileged access, secure or non-secure.

enum_values.name: Unprivileged
enum_values.description: Read and write to PWR functions can be done by privileged or unprivileged access

enum_values.name: Privileged
enum_values.description: Read and write to PWR functions can be done by privileged access only
